§6Poisonous Mud§f

§aA corrosive byproduct of the Watz Power Plant — toxic, radioactive, and surprisingly useful!§f

§bPoisonous Mud§f is a strongly corrosive, polluting, radioactive viscous liquid (NFPA 4/0/3, ACID). Produced while operating the §bWatz Power Plant§f or from processing depleted Watz pellets in a §bPUREX§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Watz Power Plant§f — produced continuously during operation
§b• PUREX§f — depleted Watz pellets + kerosene + nitric acid

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• §eCombine Steel§f — crucible: 6 nuggets of one metal + 3 nuggets of another → Combine Steel Ingot
§b• §eGas Centrifuge§f — 2 centrifuges produce: dust, lead, iron, and tiny piles of nuclear waste
§b• §eIncinerate Excess§f — mud containers hold up to 64,000 mB for easy disposal

§eTip§f: Don't throw away your poisonous mud! It's a valuable source of lead, iron, and combine steel. If truly overwhelmed, store it in mud containers and incinerate the excess. Each Watz cycle produces significant quantities, so set up a processing pipeline early!
